rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Ensure the spreadsheet export is viewable by consumers who cannot load Excel (e.g., include a formula-export sheet or CSVs). Provide the memo in full (preview was truncated).
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Both required artifacts are present. The memo includes explicit scope, inputs (R1-R3), methods, row-level citations, sensitivity checks, and a recommendation table (memo preview truncated but shows required elements). Spreadsheet file exists and is listed in actual outputs.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Make formulas explicitly visible in the workbook (or include a plain-text export of key formulas) so automated inspectors can validate lineage without additional tooling.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Memo documents method (multiplicative application of remediation percentages) and row-level inputs. Calculations in memo (per-row and aggregates) are arithmetically consistent with the stated inputs (e.g., 44*(1-0.12)=38.72, totals and post-remediation aggregates match). Spreadsheet presence confirmed, though its formulas could not be parsed in the preview environment.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: If suppression or other policy thresholds could change, include a short table showing how changing thresholds would alter flags (beyond the single sensitivity note already present).
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Policy checks were applied correctly per embedded rules: no suppression (cell_count < 5) applies to R1-R3; all epsilon_requested values > 1 are flagged for privacy review. Memo documents these flags and explains downstream effect.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Provide prioritized partial-implementation scenarios (e.g., partial scopes for O2 or staged deployments) with estimated partial costs to give actionable paths when combined options exceed budget.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Cost arithmetic and budget feasibility checks are correct and transparent. Individual costs fall under the max_budget (121,200) and pairwise/combined totals are correctly computed and shown to exceed the budget. Recommendations are ranked and sensitivity checks address budget interactions.
